title: the original plan for instrumentality
wikitext:
[]: the original plan for instrumentality             Quote Post by carla » Mon Sep 07, 2009 3:08 pm i think i asked this in some other "what was SEELE thinking?" thread, but it was a bit OT so i thought i'd ask again in a standalone thread. if i thought wrong, feel free to give me a good kick. anyway, i always sort of assumed that the idea of achieving instrumentality was SEELE'S primary goal from the beginning (and by "beginning" i mean since SEELE was formed). however, in episode 21 gendo, after yui's death (2004, then?), tells fuyutsuki-sensei that he's going to pitch a new plan to the old men: the human instrumentality project. this tells me one of two things: 1. the folks at SEELE were actually machinating instrumentality all along but gendo really had no idea they were --> in which case HAAAAAAAAAHHHH, there was something gendo didn't know! OR 2. the folks at SEELE weren't going for instrumentality to begin with, they only decided to go down that route when gendo gave them the idea --> in which case what the hell were they going for? were they just building evas and ruining lives and frying innocent women's brains just to save humanity from the angels? that doesn't sound like them at all. so, which is it, you guys? this has always confused me.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Sep 07, 2009 6:18 pm I think it was moreso that Gendo was supposing a new form of getting to the same goal. One they could pitch to the UN.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Mon Sep 07, 2009 8:03 pm I’d have to agree. His "new" plan was probably just a revision of the old one.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Tue Sep 08, 2009 8:30 am You know I never thought about that, I would assume that.............hmm..Could be an editing version of the old plan. I really don't know ;

[]:             Quote Post by Dranzer » Tue Sep 08, 2009 9:31 am Well I think that too...it was a modified and new version of the original plan... I think that,because the Seele were watching Gendo,they were afraid of him - that he betrays them with his "Modified and New" instrumentality. Well,he tried to do so,but Rei betrayed him...

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Tue Sep 08, 2009 11:33 am ...so he basically told them he was going to betray them by trying for instrumentality in his own way? they must've rejected his plan so harshly that i bet he still has nightmares. not to mention, SEELE wanted to achieve instrumentality through the evas. they were already building the evas before yui died. wouldn't that mean they went on with their original plan? *is confused*

[]:             Quote Post by Dranzer » Tue Sep 08, 2009 12:24 pm carla wrote: ...so he basically told them he was going to betray them by trying for instrumentality in his own way? they must've rejected his plan so harshly that i bet he still has nightmares. not to mention, SEELE wanted to achieve instrumentality through the evas. they were already building the evas before yui died. wouldn't that mean they went on with their original plan? *is confused*

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Sep 09, 2009 8:43 pm There was a long thread about this a while ago and I remember myself and Reichu (I think) went back and forth on it. Too bad she's gone or she might could find it as I don't remember the title. Essentially, IIRC, the primary theory is that up until that point SEELE and co. had been grasping at shadows while blindfolded in a dark room; they essentially had very little idea of exactly what they were doing (see: 2I fuck-up), so what Gendo proposed was a very clear plan of how to go about achieving their ultimate goal.

[]:             Quote Post by Mongoosedog » Wed Sep 09, 2009 9:41 pm but they would have had some idea of what they were doing right...? i mean, they were following the dead sea scrolls the whole time so whatever they said i guess that's what they were doing. i always thought that their goal was merging all life from the beginning

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Sep 09, 2009 10:43 pm They knew what they wanted but had no idea of how to go about achieving it. They knew they needed the power of Adam, but they didn't understand that power; much less how to use it. They probably knew they needed to get all life back into Lilith, but had no idea how to do it. Gendo's plan probably spelled out just how everything would work.

[]:             Quote Post by MatrixRefugee » Wed Sep 09, 2009 11:26 pm I have a feeling SEELE packaged the idea of Instrumentality as "a plan for lasting world peace" to placate the UN. Yui may have had similar ideas, or perhaps she saw it as a way of showing man what could happen if he reached for godhood, ala Icarus (Ikari-s?). Maybe Instrumentality wasn't on her mind at all, and she had the vague hope that if they could destroy the Angels, they could stop Third Impact entirely...

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Thu Sep 10, 2009 12:06 pm @yojimbo-sama-- that makes sense. just for clarification, though, when you say "the plan gendo proposed" that is SEELE's original plan, to use evas to start 3I? because i was under the impression that, while they were aware gendo had an alternate plan, they weren't sure what it was. so gendo couldn't have pitched them his plan involving Rei and lilith. is that right? uh. i just thought of something. eva-00 was the prototype, right? that would mean it was the first eva that was actually built. yet, it wasn't the first eva they put a soul into (yui died in 2004, Rei I-- whose soul is apparently in unit 00-- didn't die until 2010). why was that? why did yui not perform the contact experiment on unit 01? was there something wrong with unit 00 at the moment of the experiment, or was it that SEELE already knew eva 01, being the only lilith-based unit, was special somehow? this would imply they already had some sort of plan to use unit 01 for instrumentality. hmmm.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Sep 10, 2009 6:28 pm carla wrote: when you say "the plan gendo proposed" that is SEELE's original plan, to use evas to start 3I? because i was under the impression that, while they were aware gendo had an alternate plan, they weren't sure what it was. so gendo couldn't have pitched them his plan involving Rei and lilith. is that right?

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Thu Sep 10, 2009 8:45 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Now you're confusing me here... AFAIK, the plan Gendo gave them involved Lilith and Adam. When SEELE stopped trusting Gendo they seemed to come up with a backup plan involving the MPEs and Sho.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Sep 10, 2009 9:39 pm They probably had a good idea what Gendo's plan was. What Gendo proposed to them was likely very similar to what he planned to do himself. Instead of using Adam and Lilith to cause 3I and create Instrumentality he was going to use them to merge himself with Sho so him and Yui could be together... at least, that's the most popular theory (I think). He obviously needed Seele's support so he could run Nerv at all. When Seele stopped trusting him is when they came up with their backup plan. I don't think they cared what Gendo actually planned to do since all they knew was that Lilith and Adam was lost to them and yet they needed them or a compatible alternative.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Sep 10, 2009 11:15 pm Unit 00 was a prototype. They never actually planned to USE it until they had pretty much no choice.

[]:             Quote Post by Dranzer » Fri Sep 11, 2009 9:53 am AuraTwilight wrote: Unit 00 was a prototype. They never actually planned to USE it until they had pretty much no choice.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Fri Sep 11, 2009 11:05 am @yojimbo-san-- okay, that makes sense. although i don't particularly think "we'll let gendo have his fun for now... oh look, kaworu's dead already. quick! we've gotta start 3I before gendo can!" is a very good plan. @auratwilight-- that makes sense, as well. but then that implies that they weren't originally counting on using unit 00... were they going to put Rei in unit 01 then? is that why gendo took so damn long to call Shinji to tokyo-3, because they were planning to use Rei but it just didn't work? hmm. but then, if they weren't planning on using unit 01 for instrumentality from the beginning then... why lilith? for the sake of variety?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Sep 11, 2009 1:22 pm Well,it's a mystery about its soul...

[]:             Quote Post by Dranzer » Fri Sep 11, 2009 3:54 pm AuraTwilight wrote: What do you mean?
